title: news-scanner output
description: Canonical structured output for the news-scanner skill. Layer 1 (the data); Layer 2 (rendered stream) is described in references/rendering.md.

tier.description: Data fidelity: A=Benzinga sentiment + minute aggs, B=keyword sentiment + 5-min aggs

tier_caveats.description: Human-readable caveats (sentiment source, reaction window granularity, missing aggs)

run_at.description: UTC timestamp when the scan was run

reaction_minutes.description: Minutes after publish to measure reaction (default 60, capped at market close)

events.description: Top news events ranked by impact, descending

id.description: Massive article id, stable across publishers

ticker.description: Stock ticker for which this event is scored. One Benzinga article can produce multiple events when multi-ticker tagged.

published_at_et.description: Convenience: same timestamp formatted in US/Eastern, used in the stream header

source.description: Publisher name (Reuters, Bloomberg, Benzinga, CNBC, etc.)

headline.description: Article title. Render truncates to ~90 chars.

url.description: Canonical article URL. Used for cross-publisher dedup.

sentiment_score.description: Sentiment in [-1, +1]. Benzinga maps: positive=+0.7, neutral=0, negative=-0.7. Keyword scorer outputs continuous values.

sentiment_source.description: Which scorer produced the value. Benzinga is preferred; keyword is the fallback.

sentiment_reasoning.description: Benzinga's free-text justification for the sentiment label. Null when sentiment_source = keyword.

novelty_score.description: Cosine distance to nearest neighbor in the trailing-7-day bucket for this ticker. 1.0 = no prior coverage; 0 = exact rerun.

novelty_band.description: Banded novelty: high if score > 0.6, medium if 0.3-0.6, low if < 0.3

nearest_prior.description: The trailing-7-day article that produced the novelty distance, when one exists

reaction_pct_since_publish.description: (close at window-end / close at publish-minute) - 1. Null if publish is outside market hours and no post-open bar exists yet.

reaction_window_label.description: Human-readable label for the reaction window, e.g. '47min', '1h 7min', '3h', 'overnight'

reaction_window_minutes.description: Length of the window in minutes (capped at market close)

price_at_publish.description: Close of the bar containing publish_at_utc. Used as the reaction baseline.

price_at_window_end.description: Close of the bar at publish + reaction_minutes (capped at market close)

volume_anomaly_x.description: Avg per-minute volume during the reaction window divided by prior-5-day same-time-of-day average per-minute volume. >2x is meaningful; >5x is a spike. Null when baseline volume is unavailable.

divergence_flag.description: Flag when sentiment and reaction disagree. See references/price-news-divergence.md.

context_line.description: One-line trader context, rendered as the ↳ continuation. Examples: 'first India-specific partnership; prior coverage speculative', 'third China price cut this year; margin pressure narrative compounding'.

keywords.description: Benzinga-provided topic tags, kept for downstream filtering and clustering

related_event_ids.description: IDs of articles deduped into this event (same URL across syndicated publishers)

impact_score.description: Internal ranking score: |reaction_pct| × volume_anomaly × novelty_score. Higher = more impactful.

divergence_count.description: Total events flagged with any divergence type

take.description: One- or two-sentence read on what actually moved markets in the window. Plain prose; no bullet list.
